##NAMES=Camanchacaite
##RRUFFID=R250031
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Na[box]CaMg_2_(AsO_4_)_2_[AsO_2_(OH)_2_]
##LOCALITY=Torrecillas mine, Iquique Province, Chile
##OWNER=Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County
##SOURCE=Tony Kampf
##DESCRIPTION=Colourless, pink-beige balls with compact radial structure in association with chinchorroite (R250032), espadaite (R250051),  magnesiofluckite (R250052),  picaite (R250053) and ríosecoite (R250054).  Type sample described in Kampf et al (2019)
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral has been confirmed by X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R250031
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=(Na_0.99_Ca_0.84_Mg_2.11_Mn_0.02_)[AsO_4_][AsO_2.95_(OH)_1.05_]_2_
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Powder
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 12.47 b: 12.554 c: 6.848 alpha: 90 beta: 113.75 gamma: 90 volume: 981.3 crystal system: monoclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=532
